text: OSINTdefender: IRGC Wave 84 confirmed via IRGC press release — targets Prince Sultan (Al-Kharj) Air Base in Saudi Arabia. IRGC specifically used joint Aerospace Force + Navy operation with both solid and liquid-fuel ballistic missiles. Claiming destruction of US aerial refueling assets (KC-135 class tankers). If true, range of US F-15/F-35 sorties over Iran is cut 30-40%. CENTCOM has not confirmed or denied.
verificationNote: IRGC claim confirmed via ANI News, WANA, Tribune India. CENTCOM silence on refueling damage noted.

text: War Monitor: Cluster bomb kills 1 civilian in central Israel — Day 28 closes with Iran firing 6 missile salvos. This is Iran's 3rd confirmed cluster munition attack on Israeli population centers (after Ramat Gan March 17, Kafr Qasim March 26). Pattern: Iran is escalating indiscriminate area-effect weapons as precision guidance expended. IDF Fire and Rescue responding to 6 separate bomblet sites from the single missile. 19 total Israeli civilians killed in the conflict.
verificationNote: Cluster bomb casualty confirmed by Times of Israel Day 28 liveblog. Six separate impact sites confirmed by Israeli Fire and Rescue.

text: TankerTrackers: Day 28 Hormuz AIS summary: 0 transits confirmed (25th consecutive day below 5 transits). CSCL Indian Ocean and CSCL Arctic Ocean U-turns confirmed via AIS at ~03:20-03:50 UTC. Lotus Rising also reversed. Iran's selective transit list (7 nations) appears unenforceable — even China's COSCO can't get through. 400+ vessels at anchor, strandedseafarer count now 40,000+. Macquarie  oil 40% probability scenario by June now pricing into futures strip.
verificationNote: AIS tracking of COSCO U-turns confirmed by Bloomberg, Newsweek, FDD. Hormuz transit data from Lloyd's List.

text: Neil Miller OSINT: RUSI analysis on interceptor depletion is the most alarming strategic report of the week. Israel has exhausted most Arrow 2/3 stocks; US THAAD batteries defending GCC states are heavily degraded. Day 28 data: Israel intercepted 92% overall, but that rate is unsustainable as stockpiles thin. Arrow production line cannot outpace consumption. UK Defence Secretary Healey confirms US asked UK for interceptors — UK declined. The war is eroding the West's air defense industrial base faster than factories can replenish.
verificationNote: RUSI analysis confirmed by Haaretz. UK interceptor request confirmed by UK Defence Secretary Healey. Arrow depletion per RUSI study.

text: Conflict Monitor: Day 28 summary — Israel escalated strikes to nuclear supply chain (Ardakan yellowcake, Arak heavy water), industrial infrastructure (Mobarakeh steel), and continued largest-in-war Beirut waves. Iran responded with 6 missile salvos (including cluster warhead kills), Wave 83 + Wave 84 (84 waves in 28 days), port strikes in Kuwait, and IRGC "beyond eye for eye" threat. Trump extended energy pause to April 6 but no ceasefire. Conflict intensity at war-high. COSCO ships turned back. Brent .57 (war high). 1 Israeli civilian killed (cluster bomb). Day 28 ends most kinetically active day of the conflict.
verificationNote: Day 28 summary compiled from Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Jerusalem Post, CNBC liveblogs.

text: Houthi Watch: BREAKING — Yemen enters the 2026 Iran War. First Houthi ballistic missile attack on Israel since the Feb 28 conflict started. IDF confirms interception. This was the Houthis' own call — Al-Masirah TV says Saree made the announcement, not Tehran. Houthis may want to leverage this moment for leverage in any post-war settlement. Watch Bab al-Mandeb — if they close it, two major shipping choke points simultaneously blocked.
verificationNote: Houthi entry into war confirmed by IDF, Houthi spokesman Saree, Al Jazeera, and multiple outlets.

text: Gulf Security Monitor: Three-state simultaneous dawn attack on Gulf infrastructure — Kuwait Airport radar 'significantly damaged,' Salalah Port Oman hit (1 injured), UAE industrial zone strikes (5 injured, fires). Iran is methodically targeting civil aviation and commercial port infrastructure across multiple GCC states simultaneously. Classic force multiplication — spread air defense radars thin. Kuwait lost airport radar, which has serious implications for civil aviation safety.
verificationNote: Kuwait civil aviation confirmed radar damage. Oman News Agency confirmed Salalah port attack. UAE defense ministry confirmed industrial zone strikes.

text: Iran War Live: Day 29 summary (early): War has now spread to 5 fronts. Houthis entered. Prince Sultan confirmed: 12 US troops wounded (2 critical), E-3 AWACS damaged. Gulf: Kuwait Airport, Salalah, UAE all struck pre-dawn. Diplomatic: Pakistan hosting Saudi/Turkey/Egypt FMs — biggest peace push yet. Trump called NATO 'paper tiger.' Rubio says 'weeks not months.' RUSI warns interceptors near-critical. Interceptor rates unsustainable — Arrow 2/3 nearly depleted.
verificationNote: Day 29 early summary compiled from Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Haaretz, Washington Post, and Jerusalem Post liveblogs.

text: Aircraft Monitor: E-3 Sentry AWACS hit at Prince Sultan is operationally significant. The E-3 Sentry provides battle-space management, early warning, and command relay for all US air operations over Iran. There are roughly 32 E-3s in USAF inventory. Losing even one is not trivially replaced. Combined with the Tomahawk depletion report (850+ fired = 9x annual procurement), USAF is under genuine attrition pressure. This is why Rubio is saying 'weeks not months' — Pentagon clock is ticking on consumables.
verificationNote: E-3 Sentry AWACS damage at Prince Sultan confirmed via PBS, Washington Post. Tomahawk depletion per Washington Post (850+ fired). USAF E-3 inventory per open-source defense databases.

text: Diplomacy Watch: Islamabad summit is the most significant diplomatic event of the war so far. Saudi, Turkey, Egypt FMs physically in Pakistan — not a video call. Iran submitted 5-point counter-plan. Germany's FM Wadephul said direct US-Iran meeting in Pakistan 'prepared for very soon.' Vance named as lead negotiator. Iran's conditions (reparations, sovereignty, guarantees) are maximalist but the fact they submitted anything in writing is progress. April 6 deadline from Trump creates urgency. Window is narrow.
verificationNote: Islamabad summit confirmed by Pakistan Deputy PM Ishaq Dar. German FM Wadephul statement per Al Arabiya. Vance negotiator role per Axios. Iran's 5-point counter-plan per PBS NewsHour.

text: Conflict Monitor: Day 29 opens at maximum intensity on ALL fronts: (1) 5th front opened — Houthis enter war; (2) Gulf triple-state attack (Kuwait, Oman, UAE); (3) Prince Sultan AWACS hit confirmed; (4) Trump–NATO rupture deepens ('paper tiger'); (5) Islamabad peace summit — first real diplomatic infrastructure; (6) Interceptor stocks at critical low per RUSI. War simultaneously escalating militarily and de-escalating diplomatically. Classic endgame pressure dynamic — both sides racing to improve battlefield position before any deal.
verificationNote: Day 29 early-morning summary. All events confirmed through Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Washington Post, and Jerusalem Post liveblogs.

text: Lebanon Watch: Israel killed two journalists in Jezzine on Day 29 — Al Manar TV's Ali Shoeib and Al Mayadeen's Fatima Ftouni. IDF claims Shoeib was an IRGC intelligence operative. Lebanese President Aoun condemned the strike as an attack on press freedom. CPJ and RSF reporting at least 12 journalists killed in Lebanon since Day 1 of the conflict. Separate Bourj Hammoud apartment strike killed 1, wounded 4 this morning.
verificationNote: Journalist killings confirmed by Al Manar TV and Al Mayadeen. Lebanese President Aoun condemnation per Al Jazeera. Bourj Hammoud strike per CBS News liveblog.

text: Gulf Security Monitor: Day 29 afternoon situation report — Iran's 3-state morning attack wave on Kuwait Airport, Salalah Port, and UAE industrial zone continues Iran's deliberate pattern of striking civilian infrastructure. Aircraft radar loss at Kuwait International Airport is the most operationally significant development — civil aviation safety throughout the Gulf region may now require rerouting. IATA has issued a notice to airlines operating in the Persian Gulf airspace.
verificationNote: Kuwait Airport radar damage confirmed by Kuwait civil aviation authority. IATA notice consistent with prior Gulf airspace risk notices during conflict.

text: Islamabad Diplomacy Watch: Saudi FM Prince Faisal bin Farhan, Turkish FM Hakan Fidan, and Egyptian FM Badr Abdelatty have now all physically arrived in Islamabad — this is not a virtual call. Pakistan's Deputy PM Ishaq Dar chairing the mediation. Key question: Can these three nations deliver Iran's signature on a framework by April 6, Trump's new deadline? Iran has submitted a written 5-point counter to the US 15-point plan — the exchange of written positions through Pakistan is the most structured diplomatic process since the war began.
verificationNote: Physical presence of all three FMs in Islamabad confirmed by Pakistan government and regional media. Iran's written counter-plan confirmed by PBS NewsHour and Al Jazeera.

text: Nuclear Watchdog: The significance of the Ardakan yellowcake strike (Day 27) is still being assessed. Iran's Ardakan facility processes uranium ore concentrate — destroying it does not eliminate existing enriched stockpiles but cuts the pipeline for future enrichment feedstock. Combined with Arak heavy water reactor strike, Israel has now hit 3 distinct nodes of Iran's nuclear fuel cycle: (1) enrichment infrastructure at Fordow/Natanz, (2) Arak plutonium path, (3) Ardakan yellowcake production. IAEA reports no material was present at Ardakan during the strike, but the facility itself appears extensively damaged per satellite imagery.
verificationNote: IDF confirmed all three strikes. IAEA confirmed no nuclear material at Ardakan site during strike. ISW/Critical Threats analysis corroborated Ardakan's role in the fuel cycle.

text: Markets Pulse: Day 29 morning market summary — US equity futures are flat to slightly positive as traders balance Islamabad summit hopes against continued strikes. Brent crude holding above $112 after closing at $112.57 on Day 28. WTI still near $100 — geopolitical premium holding despite Iran toll-booth allowing some transits. Key watch: if Houthis announce Bab al-Mandeb closure alongside Hormuz, Goldman projects +$25-40/bbl immediate spike. Michigan Consumer Sentiment at 53.3 — worst since 2022.
verificationNote: Brent/WTI prices per Bloomberg. Michigan Sentiment 53.3 per University of Michigan March preliminary. Goldman scenario analysis from published research notes.

text: Iran War Live: Day 29 full morning summary — (1) Houthis enter war: first ballistic missile at Israel intercepted; (2) Iran kills 2 journalists + 1 civilian in Lebanon morning strikes; (3) Gulf triple strike (Kuwait airport radar, Salalah port, UAE) from pre-dawn; (4) Prince Sultan: 12 US wounded confirmed, AWACS damaged; (5) Islamabad summit underway — Saudi/Turkey/Egypt FMs physically present; (6) Iran holds on Hormuz: blockade entering Day 26; (7) Oil $112/bbl, gas $3.98; (8) Interceptor stocks at critical low. Next key marker: April 6 Trump deadline.
verificationNote: Day 29 morning summary compiled from Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Haaretz, Washington Post, CBS News, and Jerusalem Post liveblogs.

text: Houthi Monitor: Confirmed — Houthi entry into the 2026 Iran war is their second major military campaign against Israel, following the 2023-24 Red Sea operations. AIS analysis shows no commercial traffic in Houthi-controlled Red Sea approach. If Bab al-Mandeb closes, it joins Hormuz to block two of world's five major shipping chokepoints — roughly 35% of global traded goods.
verificationNote: Houthi spokesman confirmed attack. AIS vessel tracking data public. Bab al-Mandeb threat via Houthi deputy information minister Mohammed Mansour confirmed by Haaretz, CNBC.

text: ISW analysis (Day 29): Houthi entry into the Iran war marks a significant expansion of the conflict's geographic scope. Houthi missiles likely sourced from pre-conflict Iranian stockpile transfers. The Bab al-Mandeb threat is credible — Houthis demonstrated capability during 2023-24 Red Sea campaign. Five simultaneous military fronts is unprecedented in post-WWII Middle East conflict.
verificationNote: ISW Critical Threats daily update assessment. CNBC confirmed Houthi entry. Five simultaneous fronts assessment is ISW analytical judgment.

text: OSINTdefender: Iran fired 7 separate missile salvos at Israel on Day 29 alone — a single-day record. Iron Dome tasked with unusual ballistic missile intercepts as Arrow stocks depleted. Open-source thermal imaging shows impact sites in Tel Aviv district (Neve Tzedek area), with Israeli rescue services at 11 confirmed locations. 1 civilian killed — a man in his 50s in central Tel Aviv.
verificationNote: Seven salvos confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. Impact sites geolocated by CNN. Casualty confirmed by Haaretz. Iron Dome ballistic intercept confirmed by RUSI/Haaretz.

text: War Monitor: Vance podcast signals a shift — he is clearly being positioned as the lead Iran negotiator ahead of Islamabad talks. Iran's preference for Vance over Kushner/Witkoff tracks with Tehran's desire for higher-level engagement. Fox News poll showing 64% disapproval of Trump's Iran war handling may be accelerating US internal timeline pressure.
verificationNote: Vance podcast confirmed by Benny Johnson show, reported by Haaretz and Times of Israel. Fox News poll confirmed by Fox News live updates. Iran preference for Vance confirmed via Axios.

text: Aurora Intelligence: Lebanon toll reaches 1,189 confirmed — 47 killed in 24 hours. Three journalists killed in a single strike on a marked media vehicle in Jezzine. WHO documents 9 paramedics killed in 5 separate strikes in one day — 51 total healthcare workers killed in Lebanon. Israeli anti-war protest in Tel Aviv with 1,200 attendees and 18 arrested is the largest single-location anti-war demonstration in Israel to date.
verificationNote: Lebanon toll confirmed by Lebanese Health Ministry. Three journalists confirmed by CPJ, Washington Post, PBS. WHO paramedic figures confirmed by WHO statement. Protest figures confirmed by Haaretz and CNN.

text: Tanker Trackers: Salalah Port suspended operations after Iranian drone destroys container crane. Maersk confirmed all crew safe; suspended ops for 48h for damage assessment. AIS shows 0 vessels actively transiting Hormuz in past 6 hours. Pakistan's 20-ship agreement (2/day) is marginal — would restore <0.5% of pre-war Hormuz flow. Brent at .57 correctly pricing a prolonged disruption.
verificationNote: Salalah suspension confirmed by Maersk and Maritime Executive. Pakistan ship agreement confirmed by Washington Examiner and CNN liveblog. Brent price confirmed by Bloomberg.
